title: Non-record: 1x RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell 10L Int5-MLP (1.1935 BPB)
body:
## Summary

Adds a non-record unlimited-compute submission that ports the merged `2026-03-20_10L_Int5MLP_MuonWD04_SWA50` recipe to a single `RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition`.

Best completed run:

- exact post-quant `val_bpb`: `1.19349046`
- exact post-quant `val_loss`: `2.01515331`
- pre-quant `val_bpb`: `1.2082`
- steps: `14085`
- wallclock: `2700s`
- artifact bytes: `15691796`

## Included

- portable single-GPU `train_gpt.py` snapshot
- `results.tsv` with the completed submission run and one incomplete follow-up run
- preserved tail excerpts from both Lightning-generated logs
- `submission.json`

## Notes

- This is a non-record submission in the unlimited-compute track, not a 10-minute record claim.
- The longer follow-up run is included for context only and is not used as the submission score.

comments.body: The log label "Total submission size int8+zlib" is misleading here because the artifact produced above is logged as int6+{_COMPRESSOR} (and _COMPRESSOR may be "zstd"). Consider updating the message to reflect the actual quantization/compressor so log tails and later metadata don’t imply zlib/int8 when zstd/int6 is used.

These final evaluation log keys are named "final_int8_zlib_roundtrip" even though the roundtripped weights were loaded from the mixed int5/int6 artifact and the compressor may be zstd. Renaming the log keys (or making them depend on _COMPRESSOR / quant scheme) would avoid confusion when comparing runs.
```suggestion
    # Log final evaluation for mixed-int6 roundtripped weights, with compressor in the tag
    quant_scheme = "mixed_int6"
    compressor = _COMPRESSOR if "_COMPRESSOR" in globals() else "zlib"
    roundtrip_tag = f"final_{quant_scheme}_{compressor}_roundtrip"
    log0(
        f"{roundtrip_tag} val_loss:{q_val_loss:.4f} val_bpb:{q_val_bpb:.4f} "
        f"eval_time:{1000.0 * (time.perf_counter() - t_qeval):.0f}ms"
    )
    log0(f"{roundtrip_tag}_exact val_loss:{q_val_loss:.8f} val_bpb:{q_val_bpb:.8f}")
```

In this submission metadata, "bytes_model_int8_zlib" appears to be recording the compressed artifact size even though the included log tail reports "Serialized model int6+zstd" for the same byte count. To keep metadata unambiguous (and consistent with other submissions that use bytes_model_int6_zstd), consider renaming this field (or adding the correct one) and also populating bytes_code instead of null (it can be derived from bytes_total - bytes_model).
```suggestion
  "bytes_model_int6_zstd": 15636771,
  "bytes_code": 55025,
```

The follow-up run row shows artifact_bytes=16180242, which exceeds the repo’s stated decimal 16MB cap (16,000,000 bytes). Since this directory is under track_non_record_16mb, it would help to call this out explicitly in the row’s notes/status (or README) so readers don’t assume it was a valid under-cap artifact.
```suggestion
rtx6000_65m_b131k_s32_eb512	2.0281	1.2011			16180242	20330	3900	32	512	incomplete_eval	training finished; sliding eval was interrupted after 11.9% with running_bpb about 1.195432; artifact_bytes=16180242 exceeds 16MB cap (non-record over-cap run)
```
